title: Knights of the Old Republic 6
content:
{{Top|rwm|old|title=''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:KOTOR6cover.jpg]] |title=''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6 |writer=[[John Jackson Miller]][[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] |penciller=[[Brian Ching]] |inker= |letterer=[[Michael Heisler]] |colorist=[[Michael Atiyeh]] |cover artist=[[Travis Charest]] |editor=*[[Jeremy Barlow]] *[[Dave Marshall]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |release date=[[June 28]], [[2006]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-892/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-6|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #6}} |pages=32 |upc= |timeline=[[3964 BBY]] |series=[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] |issue=6 }} '''''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6''' is the sixth issue in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] series of comics, and the last issue in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Commencement|''Commencement'']] story arc. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=COMMENCEMENT
"PART 6 (OF SIX)" |text=After discovering his friends dead at the feet of their own Masters, Padawan ZAYNE CARRICK is blamed for the crime—along with GRYPH, a small-time Snivvian con artist. The two escape from the planet Taris, with two Arkanian refugees—the addled inventor CAMPER and his protector, the fierce JARAEL. Zayne finds a clue explaining his Masters' behavior on the ROGUE MOON, a meteor-blasted planetoid his Masters once visited with him and his felow students. The re- vived droid T1-LB (ELBEE) reveals that the Masters, while meditating, had a horrific vision—of one of their own students destroying the Jedi order. Now realizing that their pursuit of him will never stop, Zayne sees only one way out. To win his fellow fugitives' freedom, he surrenders to the bounty hunter bent on returning him to Taris—to the very Masters he believes killed his friends…}} ==Plot summary== [[Zayne Carrick|Zayne's]] return to [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] gains a lot of public attention. [[Valius Ying]], on the way to the [[Jedi Tower]], drags him through a crowd in the [[Upper City]], excited and eager to see the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] make swift justice to someone who allegedly [[Padawan Massacre|murdered five of their kind]]. When they enter the Tower, [[Lucien Draay|Lucien]] asks Valius to wait for his payment. Meanwhile, he tells Zayne the truth about what happened just before he had rushed into the ceremonial room, seeing the other four Padawans dead and the Masters having their lightsabers drawn. According to Lucien, Zayne's late arrival was expected, and the Masters were prepared to wait; it was [[Shad Jelavan]] who surprised them. Shad, seeing that Lucien was wearing his lightsaber on the supposed knighting ceremony, figured out that it meant Zayne was also going to be knighted, which surprised him because the Masters had never treated Zayne seriously. The Masters had to invent some ad hoc excuses, which made Shad even more suspicious and forced the Masters to attack prematurely—just before Zayne ran in. Throughout Lucien's narration, Valius tries to interrupt him several times, as all he wants is to get his reward and leave. Lucien eventually kills him as an unwanted witness. The Masters reveal to Zayne that they are members of the [[Jedi Covenant]], and while it is possible that Zayne is not the Sith Lord they foresaw, they are not taking any more chances. However, as the Masters are preparing to execute Zayne, the red figure from their visions bursts through the roof, wielding Zayne's lightsaber. Everyone is disoriented; the figure reveals itself to Zayne as [[Jarael]] wearing Zayne's [[Environment suit/Legends|space suit]]. Zayne collects the Masters' lightsabers with the Force and throws them into the hole in the roof as Jarael, holding him, flies through it using a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]. Three weeks later, during which riots have spread throughout Taris, the five Masters receive news they are being recalled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], noting that Taris is defenseless against the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorians]] now. Suddenly, Lucien receives a call from his former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. Zayne claims to have experienced a vision on his own, seeing that one of the Masters is going to confess and clear his name, and that he will hunt down all of them and spare the one who confesses. In the end, Zayne says that if he ends up collapsing the Jedi Order, the Masters should note that they were the ones who started it. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Knights of the Old Republic 6}} ===Cover gallery=== File:KOTOR6cover.jpg|Cover art File:SWKOTOR6-FC.jpg|Full cover art ===Collected in=== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Volume 1: Commencement]]'' *''[[Star
